articles.title: Novelis starts operating electric pusher furnace at Sierre aluminium plant
articles.summary: Novelis has commissioned a new electric pusher furnace at its Sierre, Switzerland plant, reducing CO₂ emissions by approximately 4,500 tonnes per year and cutting energy consumption by around 25% versus the gas-fired furnace it replaces.

articles.title: ASI certifies Laminazione Sottile Group against Chain of Custody Standard V2
articles.summary: The Aluminium Stewardship Initiative has certified Laminazione Sottile Group against its Chain of Custody Standard V2, recognising the Italian FRP producer's responsible sourcing and supply chain traceability practices.
articles.novelis_angle: ASI CoC V2 certification strengthens Laminazione Sottile's sustainability positioning with European packaging customers, the same segment where Novelis competes on recycled-content credentials.

articles.title: Iran ceasefire brings relief but production scars run deep
articles.summary: Gulf aluminium producers EGA and Alba sustained damage from Iranian strikes and declared force majeure; the ceasefire eases further disruption risk but production restarts are expected to take six to twelve months, sustaining tight aluminium supply to automotive rolling mills.
articles.novelis_angle: Prolonged Gulf smelter outages keep European P1020 premiums elevated and reinforce demand for Novelis's scrap-based sheet, where input costs are decoupled from primary metal price spikes.

articles.title: Raffmetal awarded by Ferrari for the value of its Silval primary alloys from recycling
articles.summary: Italian aluminium recycler Raffmetal received Ferrari's Co-Prosperity award at Podio Ferrari 2026 for its Silval 7 alloy, a 100% recycled aluminium alloy with a third-party verified carbon footprint of less than 1 kg CO₂eq/kg Al from cradle to gate.
articles.novelis_angle: A sub-1 kg CO₂eq/kg Al verified certification for 100% recycled aluminium sets a public benchmark in the automotive supply chain, directly relevant to Novelis's own recycled-content positioning with OEM customers.

articles.title: Aluminium rises as US-Iran ceasefire tested
articles.summary: LME three-month aluminium rose 0.61% to $3,156.5/t and Shanghai futures climbed 0.72% to 23,085 yuan/t after new strikes on Iranian targets renewed supply-disruption concerns; the Middle East accounts for approximately 9% of global aluminium smelting capacity.
